title: Undergraduate Admission - How to Submit Documents
content:
Queen’s University accepts self-reported (unofficial) grades for assessment purposes for all on-campus applications for September intake. Conditionally admitted applicants must have official transcripts sent before beginning studies. Official transcripts are final transcripts sent directly from your school, governing body, or test centre.

Documents can be submitted via Parchment and MyCreds. Documents submitted through Naviance are not accepted. Common App applicants must submit documents using the listed methods, as Common App submissions cannot be accessed by Queen’s.

Do not submit additional information such as passports, resumes, certifications, letters of reference/motivation/continued interest, as the Selection Committee will not consider them and will destroy them.

Do not submit documentation until the application is successfully submitted (including application fee) and an applicant ID/application number is received (issued within a week of paying the application fee on OUAC, CommonApp, or Queen’s Website).

title: Undergraduate Admission - Acceptable Submission Methods
content:
Both official and unofficial transcripts and other academic results can be submitted by student, counsellor, or agent via the online document upload form (for documents requested on a To-Do-List). Educational institutions unable to use the upload form can email transcripts to admdocs@queensu.ca (including MyCreds).

Physical copies of documents can be mailed to:
Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment
Queen's University
Gordon Hall
74 Union Street
Kingston, Ontario, Canada
K7L 3N6

Final transcripts must be received directly from school, test centre, or governing body to clear offer conditions.

title: Undergraduate Admission - Automatic Grade Submission
content:
Current Ontario, BC, and CEGEP students will have their grades submitted automatically through OUAC if:
- Quebec CEGEP code is present
- BC PEN and Student Transcript Service Connected

To be considered official, English Language Results must be sent by the testing body.

title: Undergraduate Admission - Documents in Other Languages
content:
Applicants whose documents are not in English or French must:
- Submit an official transcript(s) in the original language.
- Submit a certified translation of the transcript(s) by:
  - The Canadian consulate, high commission or embassy in the country where the documents were issued.
  - A translator accredited by a professional association of translators in Canada (e.g., ATIO, phone: 1.800.234.5030 or 613.241.2846, info@atio.on.ca).
  - COSTI Immigrant Services if ATIO cannot provide a translator (phone: 416.534.7400, ext. 222, fax: 416.534.2482, costi.org).
  - A translator accredited through federal, provincial, or municipal government in Canada.

The translator must include:
- A photocopy of the document(s) used for translation
- An original statement indicating:
  - Accuracy and authenticity of translation
  - Translator belongs to one of the approved categories (with identification number/seal, name, address, telephone)
  - Printed name and signature of translator

title: Undergraduate Admission - Additional Notes
content:
After documentation has been received, it can take 3 to 5 business days for your To-Do List to update.

Do not email if a Queen’s transcript appears on your To-Do List.

Documents submitted to Queen's University become the property of the University and will not be returned. Queen's may request original documents and/or submit documents for verification to the issuing institutions at its discretion.

title: Unofficial Documents Guidelines - Overview
content:
Queen’s University expects applicants to submit official documents whenever possible. In extenuating or exceptional circumstances, unofficial documents may be considered.

If admission is granted based on unofficial documents, the offer of admission will be conditional. An official transcript must be submitted by the deadline listed in the SOLUS Student Centre. Failure to provide an official transcript by the deadline will result in the offer of admission being revoked.

title: Unofficial Documents Guidelines - Required Information
content:
Unofficial transcripts must include:
- Applicant’s full name
- Name of the school, university, or college attended
- A complete list of courses attempted
- Credit value for each course
- Grades awarded
- The institution’s grading scale
- Graduation credential and date issued (if awarded)

title: Unofficial Documents Guidelines - Acceptable Unofficial Documents
content:
Unofficial documents may include:
- A copy of an official transcript issued by an accredited university or college
- A transcript obtained from a self-serve university or college web system
- A copy of a high school credit counselling summary or equivalent
- A copy of an official high school transcript issued by a school, school board, Ministry of Education, or examination body
- World Education Services (WES) credential authentication
- A copy of an unofficial exam result (e.g., SAT, ACT, AP)
- A copy of a Language Proficiency Test Score (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL, Duolingo, CAEL, PTE Academic)

title: Unofficial Documents Guidelines - Ontario, BC, and Quebec Applicants
content:
All course and grade information for current Ontario secondary school applicants must be submitted through the Ontario Universities’ Application Centre (OUAC). Unofficial transcripts submitted by current Ontario secondary school applicants will not be considered.

British Columbia curriculum applicants and Quebec CÉGEP applicants must arrange for course and grade information to be transmitted electronically via OUAC. Unofficial transcripts submitted by current BC curriculum applicants and Quebec CÉGEP applicants will not be considered.

title: Unofficial Documents Guidelines - Submission Rules and Responsibilities
content:
Self-reported grades will not be considered.

Documents submitted in PDF format must be uploaded through the Document Submission Form.

Queen’s University reserves the right to request an official transcript at any point during the admission process.

Applicants are responsible for ensuring that all application information and supporting documentation is truthful, complete, and accurate. Applicants are encouraged to review the Statement of Application Fraud for further details.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Overview
content: Transfer credit assessment takes place after a student has accepted an offer of admission. Transfer credit assessment outcomes are not appealable. There may be a minimum grade threshold before transfer credit will be granted.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - High School Credits
content: Eligible students who do not submit a final transcript and/or course descriptions (where required) by the end of their first term as a Queen's student are considered to have declined transfer credit.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - High School Credits Assessment
content: Transfer credit assessment takes place after a student has accepted an offer of admission.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - High School Credits Grades
content: There may be a minimum grade threshold before transfer credit will be granted. Achieved grades from transfer credits will not be reflected on a Queen’s transcript – only the designation "TR".

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Post-Secondary Credits Disclosure
content: Students who have taken post-secondary credit prior to attending Queen’s must disclose this information on the OUAC application form. Failure to disclose all post-secondary studies during the time of application is considered Academic Dishonesty and may prohibit an applicant from receiving transfer credit or may result in withdrawal from the University.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Post-Secondary Credits Documentation
content: Detailed, electronic course descriptions are required for a transfer credit assessment to be completed. Queen's will not request course descriptions on students' behalf from their previous institution. Only courses for which course descriptions have been received can be assessed for transfer credit.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Course Description Language
content: Course descriptions must be submitted in English, except for "Language" courses, where the syllabi will be accepted in the language of instruction.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Invalid Submissions
content: Paper-based outlines mailed to the Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment office are not accepted for transfer credit review.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Submission Deadlines
content: Eligible students who do not submit a final transcript and/or course descriptions (where required) by the end of their first term as a Queen's student are considered to have declined transfer credit.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Minimum Grade Requirements
content: Credit is not granted for courses with a final grade lower than 63% or C. Credit is not granted for courses which receive a pass/fail grade.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Assessment Authority
content: Transfer credit assessments are completed by the Undergraduate Chair in the department most relevant to the course content.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Appeals
content: Credit assessment outcomes are not appealable.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Credit Limits
content: Transfer credit limits are imposed by each School or Faculty. Students must complete the majority of the academic work required for both their degree program and their concentration through Queen's University. Please refer to the Academic Calendar of your School or Faculty for additional information.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Transcript Notation
content: Achieved grades from transfer credits will not be reflected on a Queen’s transcript, only the designation "TR".

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Finality of Transfer Credits
content: Once applied to a student account, transfer credit will not be removed by the Undergraduate Admission and Recruitment Office.

title: Transfer Credit Policies - Degree Completion Requirement
content: Students must complete the majority of the academic work required for both their degree program and their concentration through Queen's University.
